name: API Gateway Pattern
strategicIntent: Provide a centralized, secure API gateway for all consumer-facing and partner APIs, enforcing consistent authentication, rate-limiting, routing, and API lifecycle management across McCain's digital ecosystem.
problemStatement: Without a centralized gateway, API consumers connect directly to backend services, leading to inconsistent authentication enforcement, no rate-limiting, difficulty in API versioning, and inability to monitor API usage patterns across the enterprise.

useCases.use: External partner APIs for order management and supply chain integration
useCases.dontUse: Internal service-to-service calls within a single Kubernetes cluster

useCases.use: Mobile and web BFF endpoints requiring OAuth2 token validation
useCases.dontUse: Batch file transfers or SFTP-based integrations

useCases.use: Public developer portal APIs with tiered rate-limiting
useCases.dontUse: Low-latency event streaming where gateway hop adds unacceptable latency

components: Azure API Management — gateway runtime, policy engine, and developer portal
Azure AD B2C — consumer and partner identity federation
Azure Key Vault — API signing keys and certificate storage
Azure Monitor & Application Insights — API analytics, latency tracking, and alerting
Azure Front Door — global load balancing and WAF for API endpoints
interactionSteps: API consumer authenticates via OAuth2 / Azure AD B2C and obtains JWT token
Request hits Azure Front Door for global routing and WAF inspection
API Management validates JWT, applies rate-limit and quota policies
Gateway routes request to backend service based on URL path and version
Response transformation policies normalize backend responses to API contract
Application Insights captures request telemetry and latency metrics
API Management publishes usage analytics to Azure Monitor dashboards
securityConsiderations: All API endpoints must require OAuth2 bearer token validation
Rate-limiting policies must be applied per subscription tier
Backend services must not be directly accessible — gateway is the sole ingress
API keys must be rotated every 90 days via Key Vault
WAF rules must be enabled on Azure Front Door for OWASP Top 10 protection
Mutual TLS required for partner-grade API integrations

guardrails.rule: All APIs must enforce OAuth2 authentication at the gateway
Rate-limiting must be configured per API product tier
All APIs must be registered in the API catalog before production deployment
APIs must support at least one prior version during deprecation window

exceptionProcess: Submit API Architecture Review request with justification for direct backend exposure. Requires Integration Architecture Board and CISO approval. Compensating controls must include WAF, IP whitelisting, and enhanced logging.

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Initial API gateway pattern with basic routing and auth

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Added rate-limiting tiers and developer portal integration

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Added Azure Front Door WAF, mutual TLS for partners, and API versioning policy

data.specs: HSM-backed keys for API signing certificates and TLS private keys; Soft-delete: 90 days; Purge protection: enabled; 90-day automatic key rotation via Key Vault rotation policy; Certificate auto-renewal for APIM custom domain TLS; RBAC authorization model (vault access policies disabled); Private endpoint connected from snet-privateendpoints-cac; Geo-redundant by default within region pair
data.justification: HSM-backed Premium tier required for APIM TLS private key storage per McCain's ISO 27001 cryptographic key management policy. Key rotation automation via Key Vault rotation policy satisfies the 90-day API key rotation guardrail without manual intervention. RBAC model enforces least-privilege — APIM system-assigned managed identity has 'Key Vault Secrets User' role only; no human operators have data plane access in production without PIM elevation.

data.sku: Pay-As-You-Go with Commitment Tier — 100 GB/day reservation
data.specs: Centralized workspace in hub subscription; Retention: 90 days hot (included), 2 years archived (cold tier); Tables: APIM gateway logs, Application Insights traces, Front Door access logs, AAD B2C audit logs, Azure Firewall logs; Data export rule to Azure Storage for long-term compliance archival (7 years for food safety audit trail); Workspace-based Application Insights integration
data.justification: 100 GB/day commitment tier provides ~30% discount vs PAYG for McCain's estimated log volume from APIM (gateway logs, policy traces), Front Door (access logs), and B2C (audit logs). Single centralized workspace enables cross-service correlation queries (KQL) across API transactions — required for SOC 2 audit evidence generation. 2-year archive retention satisfies food industry regulatory audit trail requirements at low cold tier cost (~$0.02/GB/month).

data.sku: Workspace-based (linked to central Log Analytics Workspace)
data.specs: Connected to Log Analytics workspace la-mccain-hub-cac; APIM diagnostics configured to send all gateway request/response metadata (excluding body for PII compliance); Sampling: Adaptive sampling enabled with minimum 10% floor to ensure low-volume API visibility; Custom dimensions: API name, operation, subscription ID, backend latency, policy latency; Availability tests: 5 synthetic ping tests per region testing APIM health endpoints; Smart Detection enabled
data.justification: Workspace-based Application Insights eliminates duplicate log ingestion cost by sharing the Log Analytics workspace storage. Adaptive sampling reduces telemetry cost for high-volume APIs while preserving 100% sampling for error and latency outlier events. Custom dimensions (API name, subscription tier, backend) enable the usage analytics dashboards required by the API productization pattern (IN-003) to track per-product consumption and SLA adherence.

data.specs: Used for APIM developer portal static content, API documentation media, and Log Analytics long-term export archive; Blob storage tier: Hot for developer portal assets, Archive for compliance exports >90 days; Capacity: ~500 GB estimated; TLS 1.2 minimum enforced; Public blob access disabled; Private endpoint from snet-privateendpoints-cac; Immutable blob storage policy enabled on compliance archive container (WORM, 7-year lock)
data.justification: GRS replication for production ensures developer portal availability during regional outage. Immutable WORM policy on compliance archive satisfies food safety audit trail immutability requirements — prevents deletion or modification of API transaction logs required for regulatory review. Archive tier for logs >90 days reduces compliance storage cost by ~90% vs Hot tier.

compute.sku: Premium v2 — 4 units (Canada Central primary), 2 units (Canada East secondary), 1 unit each (West Europe, Australia East)
compute.specs: Premium tier: 4 vCores + 16 GB RAM per unit; Internal VNet mode; 8 units total capacity across 4 regions; Developer portal included; 99.99% SLA with multi-region; Self-hosted gateway capability for OT/edge scenarios; Support for 10,000 req/sec per unit at P99 < 50ms
compute.justification: Premium tier is the only APIM SKU supporting internal VNet integration (required for Zero Trust backend isolation), multi-region deployment (required for EU/APAC data residency), availability zone support, and custom domain with mutual TLS. 4 units at primary region handles McCain's projected API traffic of ~30M calls/day with headroom. Reserved capacity (1-year) reduces unit cost by ~35% vs PAYG. Canada East secondary uses 2 units (warm standby) to reduce cost while meeting RTO/RPO targets.

compute.specs: Global anycast PoPs; WAF engine included; Private Link origin support (connects to APIM internal VIP); Custom domains up to 500; 100 routing rules; DDoS Standard protection included; Bot protection ruleset; 99.99% SLA
compute.justification: Premium tier is required for Private Link-based origin connection to APIM internal mode endpoints and for managed WAF ruleset access including bot protection. Provides sub-20ms routing decisions at global edge. WAF in Prevention mode with OWASP 3.2 ruleset satisfies the OWASP Top 10 guardrail without requiring a separate Application Gateway. Cost includes estimated 5TB/month egress and 250M routing decisions.

compute.sku: Self-Hosted Gateway (container, included in APIM Premium license)
compute.specs: Docker/Kubernetes container deployed on-premises at McCain manufacturing sites; Connects to APIM Premium control plane in Canada Central; Enables local API routing for OT/MES systems without data leaving site network; Supports air-gapped operation for up to 24 hours; Deployed on existing on-premises Kubernetes clusters (AKS Arc or vanilla K8s)
compute.justification: OT/IT convergence requirement: Manufacturing execution systems, SCADA interfaces, and factory floor APIs at McCain's 35+ sites require low-latency local processing and cannot route through cloud gateway for real-time control loops. Self-hosted gateway extends APIM policy enforcement (JWT validation, rate-limiting) to on-premises without additional licensing cost. Supports up to 25 self-hosted gateway nodes per APIM instance.

compute.sku: Premium P2 (linked to APIM B2C tenant)
compute.specs: Up to 50,000 MAU included in base; $0.0165/MAU above 50K; Custom policies (IEF) for partner SAML/OIDC federation; MFA included; Conditional Access for high-risk consumer sessions; Identity Protection for B2C included in P2
compute.justification: B2C P2 provides Identity Protection and Conditional Access for consumer and partner APIs, enabling risk-based token issuance that feeds back to APIM JWT validation policy. Estimated 20,000-50,000 MAU for consumer and partner API users across McCain's digital channels. P2 required for Conditional Access integration with APIM subscription policies.

azureDesign.overview:
This Azure Technical Design Document defines the centralized API Gateway architecture for McCain Foods (Pattern AP-001), providing a secure, observable, and globally distributed API management platform across McCain's digital ecosystem. The design addresses the core problem of fragmented API consumption by introducing Azure API Management (APIM) as the sole ingress point for all consumer-facing and partner-grade APIs, eliminating direct backend exposure and enforcing consistent authentication, rate-limiting, versioning, and telemetry collection enterprise-wide. The solution is aligned with McCain's existing hub-spoke network topology and ExpressRoute connectivity model, ensuring that all API traffic — whether originating from retail partners, mobile consumers, or internal manufacturing systems — flows through a governed, policy-enforced gateway layer before reaching any downstream service.

The architecture leverages Azure Front Door Premium as the global anycast entry point, providing geographic load balancing, TLS termination, and OWASP Top 10 WAF protection across McCain's North American, European, and Asia-Pacific regions. Azure API Management (Premium tier) is deployed in internal VNet integration mode across two paired Azure regions (Canada Central as primary, Canada East as secondary), with additional gateway units deployed in West Europe and Australia East to satisfy data residency requirements for McCain's European and Asia-Pacific operations. Azure AD B2C federates consumer and partner identities, issuing OAuth2 JWT tokens that APIM validates at the gateway edge via JWT validation policies before any request reaches a backend service.

Security controls are built on Zero Trust principles, with all backend services accessible exclusively through APIM using private endpoints or VNet-integrated service connections. API signing certificates and subscription keys are stored and rotated via Azure Key Vault with 90-day automated rotation policies. Mutual TLS is enforced at the APIM policy level for all partner-tier API products. Azure Defender for APIs (part of Defender for Cloud) provides continuous threat detection across the APIM surface, and all diagnostic logs, gateway metrics, and Application Insights telemetry are streamed to a centralized Log Analytics workspace in McCain's hub subscription for unified observability.

This design satisfies McCain's regulatory obligations under SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and food safety data governance requirements by enforcing data residency through region-specific APIM gateway units, maintaining comprehensive audit logs of all API transactions, and enabling fine-grained access control via APIM subscription products and Entra ID RBAC. The design is expressed entirely as Infrastructure-as-Code using the McCain CCoE Terraform module (github.com/mccain/terraform-azure-apim-gateway) and governed by the Azure Policy definition Require-APIM-For-External-APIs, which prevents any external-facing service from being deployed without a registered APIM gateway in front of it.

security.waf: Azure Front Door Premium WAF Policy (waf-mccain-apim-prod) configured in Prevention mode with: (1) OWASP Core Rule Set 3.2 — all rules enabled, custom exclusions documented for known false positives in McCain's food catalogue API (exclusions require CCoE approval and 90-day review cycle); (2) Microsoft Bot Protection ruleset — blocks malicious bots, allows verified partners; (3) Rate limiting rule: 1,000 requests/5-minutes per client IP as coarse-grained DDoS guard at edge (fine-grained rate limiting handled at APIM product/subscription level); (4) Geo-filtering: Block requests from OFAC-sanctioned countries at Front Door edge; (5) Custom rule: Reject requests without valid User-Agent header (eliminates basic scripted abuse). DDoS Network Protection plan (not Standard — Network Protection at VNet level) attached to APIM spoke VNets in Canada Central and West Europe. APIM inbound policy validates X-Azure-FDID header against Front Door instance ID to prevent WAF bypass via direct-to-APIM-VIP attacks.
security.defender: Microsoft Defender for Cloud enabled at 'McCain-Platform' management group level with the following plans active on APIM subscription: (1) Defender for APIs (GA) — continuously monitors APIM endpoints for anomalous call patterns, shadow API detection, and API-specific OWASP threats; generates security alerts fed to Microsoft Sentinel; (2) Defender for App Service — if APIM developer portal is hosted on App Service plan; (3) Defender for Key Vault — monitors for unusual access patterns, PIM bypass attempts, and mass secret enumeration; (4) Defender CSPM (Cloud Security Posture Management) — generates secure score recommendations for APIM TLS configuration, network exposure, and diagnostic settings gaps. Defender for APIs API inventory is reconciled monthly against the APIM API catalog as part of the api-gateway-operations-runbook-v2.1 governance process. All Defender alerts route to the McCain SOC via Sentinel data connector.
security.identity: Entra ID (corporate) and Azure AD B2C (consumer/partner) are the dual identity planes. APIM system-assigned managed identity is used for all service-to-service authentication (Key Vault secret reads, Application Insights write, Log Analytics ingestion) — no stored credentials anywhere. APIM named values reference Key Vault secrets directly via managed identity. Corporate API developers and operations engineers access APIM management plane via Entra ID RBAC with roles: 'API Management Service Contributor' (CCoE Integration team), 'API Management API Contributor' (application teams — publish APIs only), 'API Management Service Reader' (read-only for auditors). All privileged APIM management operations require PIM (Privileged Identity Management) just-in-time elevation with 4-hour maximum session, MFA, and justification. Azure AD B2C issues OAuth2 JWT tokens with claims mapping API subscription tier, tenant ID, and allowed API scopes. APIM validate-jwt inbound policy enforces token signature (RS256), issuer (B2C tenant endpoint), audience (APIM service client ID), and expiry on every request. Token lifetime: 1 hour access token, 24-hour refresh token for consumer apps; 30-minute access token with client_credentials flow for partner M2M integrations.
security.keyVault: Azure Key Vault Premium (kv-mccain-apim-prod-cac) stores: (1) APIM custom domain TLS certificates for *.api.mccainfoods.com — auto-renewed 30 days before expiry via Key Vault + DigiCert integration; (2) API signing keys for JWT validation — RSA 4096-bit HSM-backed, 90-day rotation via Key Vault rotation policy with EventGrid notification to APIM for named value refresh; (3) Backend service API keys for APIM-to-backend authentication (mutual auth supplement); (4) B2C token signing key references. Key Vault RBAC: APIM managed identity = 'Key Vault Secrets User' + 'Key Vault Certificate User'; CCoE team = 'Key Vault Secrets Officer' (PIM-gated); No 'Key Vault Administrator' assigned permanently — break-glass procedure documented in CCoE runbook api-gateway-operations-runbook-v2.1. Soft-delete (90 days) and purge protection enabled — prevents accidental deletion causing APIM outage. Key Vault private endpoint in snet-privateendpoints-cac; all access logs streamed to Log Analytics.
security.additionalNotes:
Mutual TLS (mTLS) for partner-grade APIs: APIM configured with client certificate authentication for all API products in the 'Partner' subscription tier. Partner certificates issued by McCain's intermediate CA (stored in Key Vault) or accepted from partner-provided CA certificates on a per-partner approved list. APIM inbound policy uses check-client-certificate policy to validate certificate thumbprint against an allowed list stored in APIM named values (sourced from Key Vault). Certificate thumbprint rotation coordinated via partner onboarding process documented in CCoE runbook.

API Key Rotation: APIM subscription keys for non-OAuth2 legacy consumers (internal systems) auto-rotated via Azure Function triggered by Key Vault rotation event every 90 days. Consumers notified via API Management subscription email 30 days before rotation. Dual key slots (primary/secondary) in APIM prevent rotation-induced downtime — secondary activated before primary rotation.

Penetration Testing: APIM endpoints included in McCain's quarterly API penetration testing scope per SOC 2 CC7.1 control. Front Door WAF logs reviewed post-pentest to validate rule coverage.

Threat Model: STRIDE analysis maintained in McCain's security architecture repository, covering: Spoofing (mitigated by JWT validation + mTLS), Tampering (mitigated by HTTPS-only + response integrity headers), Repudiation (mitigated by comprehensive APIM diagnostic logging), Information Disclosure (mitigated by private endpoints + no body logging for PII APIs), Denial of Service (mitigated by Front Door rate limiting + APIM quotas), Elevation of Privilege (mitigated by APIM product tier isolation + Entra ID RBAC).
security.privateEndpoints: All data-plane service communications use Azure Private Endpoints, eliminating public internet exposure for backend services. Private endpoints provisioned: (1) kv-mccain-apim-prod-cac → privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net → 10.10.3.4; (2) storage account developer portal → privatelink.blob.core.windows.net → 10.10.3.5; (3) Application Insights ingestion → privatelink.applicationinsights.azure.com → 10.10.3.6; (4) Log Analytics workspace → privatelink.ods.opinsights.azure.com → 10.10.3.7. APIM internal mode VNet integration ensures the gateway itself has no public endpoint for the API runtime — gateway traffic enters only via Front Door Private Link origin (AFD ↔ APIM VIP). APIM management plane endpoint (management.azure-api.net) exposed only to ApiManagement service tag per NSG rules. Backend API services (Azure Functions, AKS, App Service) accessed via their own private endpoints or VNet integration — never via public internet. Azure Policy 'Deny-Public-IP-On-Backend-Services' enforced at subscription level.

monitoring.alerts:
Azure Monitor alert rules configured with action group ag-mccain-apim-oncall (PagerDuty integration for P1/P2, email for P3/P4):

P1 - Critical (PagerDuty immediate page):
• APIM Gateway Availability < 99% over 5-minute window (metric: Requests, split by ResponseCode=5xx > 10% of total)
• APIM backend connectivity failure — all backends in a region returning 5xx for > 3 minutes
• Key Vault secret retrieval failures > 5 in 1 minute (indicates rotation failure or access issue)
• Front Door WAF block rate spike > 500% above baseline (potential DDoS)

P2 - High (PagerDuty 15-min SLA):
• APIM P99 latency > 3,000ms for any API product over 10-minute window
• APIM capacity utilization > 80% (units approaching saturation — trigger to scale up)
• B2C token issuance failure rate > 5% over 15 minutes
• API subscription quota exhaustion for any production subscription

P3 - Medium (email + Teams notification):
• APIM certificate expiry < 30 days (pre-rotation warning)
• APIM backend P95 latency > 1,000ms
• JWT validation failure rate > 1% (potential misconfigured consumer)
• Log Analytics ingestion volume > 150 GB/day (budget alert)

P4 - Low (daily digest email):
• New API registered without required tags (via Azure Policy audit event)
• Deprecated API version still receiving traffic 30 days post-deprecation notice
• APIM developer portal availability check failure (synthetic monitor)

Monthly SLA report generated via Azure Monitor Workbook and emailed to CCoE Integration team and application owners.
monitoring.appInsights: Application Insights resource (appi-mccain-apim-prod-cac) configured as workspace-based, linked to Log Analytics workspace la-mccain-hub-cac. APIM diagnostic settings push the following to Application Insights: all gateway request/response logs (headers only, body logging disabled for PII compliance on consumer APIs — enabled selectively on internal/partner APIs with data classification 'Internal' or below), backend latency, policy execution duration, error codes, and subscription ID. Custom dashboard 'APIM API Health' built in Azure Workbooks showing: P50/P95/P99 latency per API operation, error rate by API and subscription tier, top 10 consumers by call volume, quota utilization per product, and backend SLA adherence. Distributed tracing: correlation IDs injected by APIM into backend requests via set-header policy (X-Correlation-Id), enabling end-to-end traces from Front Door → APIM → backend service in Application Insights Transaction Search. Live Metrics stream enabled for real-time traffic visibility during incident response. Smart Detection rules active for: request failure rate anomalies, latency degradation, and dependency call failure spikes.
monitoring.logAnalytics: Single centralized Log Analytics workspace (la-mccain-hub-cac, workspace ID stored as APIM named value) in hub subscription. All APIM instances across 4 regions ship diagnostic logs to this workspace via Azure Monitor diagnostic settings — log categories enabled: GatewayLogs, DeveloperPortalAuditLogs, WebSocketConnectionLogs. Retention policy: 90 days interactive, 2-year archive (cold tier). Key KQL queries saved as workspace functions: (1) 'APIMErrorRate' — hourly error rate by API and region; (2) 'APIMQuotaExhaustion' — subscriptions approaching quota limits; (3) 'APIMBackendLatency' — P95 backend response time by backend host; (4) 'APIMSecurityEvents' — JWT validation failures, certificate validation failures, WAF blocks correlated with APIM logs; (5) 'APIMDataResidency' — verifies EU requests only hit West Europe gateway unit (compliance query). Front Door WAF logs, B2C audit logs, Key Vault audit logs, and Azure Firewall logs also ingest to same workspace enabling cross-service security correlation. Log Analytics data export rule ships all logs to Azure Storage immutable container for 7-year compliance archival.
monitoring.additionalNotes:
APIM Named Values audit: Monthly automated check (Logic App) verifies all APIM named values sourced from Key Vault are resolving correctly — alerts if Key Vault secret reference returns 404 (deleted or rotated without APIM update).

Capacity Planning: APIM capacity metric (0-100 scale) monitored with 90-day trend analysis. Autoscale not natively supported in APIM Premium — capacity alerts at 70% trigger a manual approval workflow (Azure DevOps pipeline) to add gateway units via Terraform. Forecast dashboard shows projected capacity exhaustion date based on 30-day growth trend.

API Versioning Compliance Monitor: Weekly Log Analytics query checks all APIs in APIM catalog for presence of at least N-1 version — generates compliance report against the 'Recommended' guardrail for version deprecation window. Non-compliant APIs flagged to API owners via automated email from Logic App.

Front Door + APIM Correlation: Front Door logs (FrontdoorAccessLog) joined with APIM GatewayLogs on X-Azure-FDID and client IP to provide end-to-end request visibility including CDN cache hit/miss status, WAF rule triggers, and APIM policy latency breakdown in a single KQL query published in the APIM Operations Workbook.

vnets.name: vnet-mccain-hub-cac-001
vnets.purpose: Hub VNet in hub-spoke topology hosting ExpressRoute gateway, Azure Firewall, shared DNS resolvers, and centralized egress. Peered to all spoke VNets including APIM spoke.

vnets.name: vnet-mccain-apim-spoke-cac-001
vnets.purpose: Primary APIM spoke VNet hosting APIM Premium internal mode VNet integration subnets, private endpoints for Key Vault and backend services, and APIM developer portal subnet

vnets.name: vnet-mccain-apim-spoke-cae-001
vnets.purpose: Secondary APIM spoke VNet for Canada East geo-redundant APIM gateway unit, peered to Canada East hub VNet

vnets.name: vnet-mccain-apim-spoke-weu-001
vnets.purpose: European APIM spoke VNet for APIM West Europe gateway unit, satisfying EU data residency; peered to West Europe hub

vnets.name: vnet-mccain-apim-spoke-aue-001
vnets.purpose: APAC APIM spoke VNet for Australia East gateway unit; peered to Australia East hub VNet

subnets.nsg: Inbound: Allow TCP 443 from AzureFrontDoor.Backend service tag; Allow TCP 3443 from ApiManagement service tag (management plane); Allow TCP 6390 from AzureLoadBalancer. Outbound: Allow TCP 443 to backends via private endpoints; Allow TCP 443 to AzureActiveDirectory, AzureKeyVault, Storage service tags; Deny all other outbound. NSG name: nsg-apim-gateway-cac
subnets.name: snet-apim-gateway-cac
subnets.purpose: Dedicated subnet for Azure API Management Premium internal VNet mode — requires /27 minimum. Hosts APIM gateway runtime NICs.

subnets.nsg: Inbound: Allow TCP 443 from VirtualNetwork; Allow TCP 53 UDP from VirtualNetwork (DNS). Outbound: Allow TCP 443 to AzureActiveDirectory. Deny all other. NSG name: nsg-apim-portal-cac
subnets.name: snet-apim-portal-cac
subnets.purpose: Subnet for APIM developer portal and management endpoint private DNS resolution; also hosts private DNS resolver inbound endpoint

subnets.nsg: Inbound: Allow TCP 443 from snet-apim-gateway-cac (10.10.1.0/27); Allow TCP 1433 from snet-apim-gateway-cac for SQL backends. Deny all other inbound. Outbound: Allow all to VirtualNetwork. NSG name: nsg-privateendpoints-cac
subnets.name: snet-privateendpoints-cac
subnets.purpose: Private endpoints for Key Vault, Storage Account, Application Insights ingestion, and backend service private links. Network policies disabled for PE attachment.

subnets.nsg: No NSG (Azure requirement for GatewaySubnet). Route table: UDR propagation from ExpressRoute BGP routes enabled.
subnets.name: snet-expressroute-gateway
subnets.purpose: GatewaySubnet for ExpressRoute Virtual Network Gateway — required naming convention, cannot have NSG attached

subnets.nsg: No NSG (Azure Firewall subnet requirement). All spoke traffic routed through 10.0.2.4 (Azure Firewall private IP) via UDR.
subnets.name: AzureFirewallSubnet
subnets.purpose: Azure Firewall Premium subnet for centralized egress filtering and IDPS — required naming, no NSG

subnets.nsg: Mirror of nsg-apim-gateway-cac with Canada East region service tag equivalents. NSG name: nsg-apim-gateway-cae
subnets.name: snet-apim-gateway-cae
subnets.purpose: Canada East APIM gateway subnet for Premium geo-redundant unit

subnets.nsg: Mirror of nsg-apim-gateway-cac with West Europe service tag scoping. NSG name: nsg-apim-gateway-weu
subnets.name: snet-apim-gateway-weu
subnets.purpose: West Europe APIM gateway subnet for EU region gateway unit

subnets.nsg: Mirror of nsg-apim-gateway-cac with Australia East service tag scoping. NSG name: nsg-apim-gateway-aue
subnets.name: snet-apim-gateway-aue
subnets.purpose: Australia East APIM gateway subnet for APAC region gateway unit

networking.additionalNotes:
DNS: Azure Private DNS Resolver deployed in hub VNet (Canada Central) with inbound endpoint at 10.0.4.4 and forwarding ruleset pushing *.azure-api.net, *.vault.azure.net, *.blob.core.windows.net, and *.monitor.azure.com to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16) for private endpoint resolution. On-premises DNS forwarders at McCain manufacturing sites configured to forward cloud-domain queries to the Private DNS Resolver inbound endpoint via ExpressRoute. Private DNS zones registered: privatelink.azure-api.net, privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net, privatelink.monitor.azure.com, privatelink.applicationinsights.azure.com — all linked to APIM spoke VNets.

VNet Peering: All APIM spoke VNets peer to their regional hub VNets with 'Allow Gateway Transit' enabled on hub side and 'Use Remote Gateways' on spoke side, enabling APIM internal mode instances to reach on-premises backends via ExpressRoute without requiring separate ExpressRoute gateways per spoke.

ExpressRoute: Existing ExpressRoute circuits at Canada Central and West Europe hub VNets carry private peering routes for 10.10.0.0/16 and 10.20.0.0/16 respectively, allowing APIM to forward requests to on-premises manufacturing execution systems (MES) and SAP backends. BGP communities used to tag APAC routes for circuit selection.

Route Tables (UDR): All APIM gateway subnets have a UDR with default route 0.0.0.0/0 next-hop set to Azure Firewall (10.0.2.4 in respective hub) to force all outbound APIM traffic through centralized IDPS inspection. Exception: /27 direct routes for APIM management plane (ApiManagement service tag) bypass firewall per Azure APIM VNet integration requirements.

Front Door to APIM Origin: Azure Front Door Premium uses the APIM's public VIP (exposed in internal mode via a dedicated public IP for Front Door health probes and AFD-sourced traffic) as origin. Backend origins are locked to accept connections only from AzureFrontDoor.Backend service tag — enforced in APIM inbound policy via check-header policy validating X-Azure-FDID header against the specific Front Door instance ID (stored as named value in APIM from Key Vault). This prevents WAF bypass.

costGuidance.notes: APIM Developer tier (1 unit, Canada Central only, no SLA, no VNet integration — uses service endpoint for backend access in dev); Azure Front Door Standard (not Premium — no WAF in dev to reduce cost); B2C Free tier (up to 50K MAU); Key Vault Standard (not Premium HSM in dev); Log Analytics reduced retention (30 days); Application Insights with 5 GB/day free tier. Dev APIM does not use internal VNet mode — simplified networking reduces dev environment complexity. Total savings vs production: ~95% cost reduction. Dev/Test subscription eligible for Azure Dev/Test pricing (-40% on APIM Developer tier). Important: Developer tier has no SLA and MUST NOT be used for any production or staging traffic.

APIM Standard v2 tier (2 units, Canada Central, internal VNet mode supported in v2, 99.95% SLA) — Standard v2 is the recommended pre-production tier as it supports VNet integration and policies but at lower cost than Premium; Azure Front Door Premium (1/4 of production WAF rule complexity); Key Vault Premium (HSM-backed to test rotation workflows accurately); Full Log Analytics workspace at reduced 50 GB/day commitment tier; B2C P1 (not P2 — Conditional Access not tested in staging). Staging environment runs weekly load tests to validate APIM capacity model for production sizing. No multi-region deployment in staging — single Canada Central region only. Estimated at 35-40% of production cost.

Cost breakdown: APIM Premium 8 units across 4 regions ~$12,000-$14,000/mo (1-year Reserved Capacity applied at primary regions for ~35% saving); Front Door Premium ~$1,200-$2,000/mo; Log Analytics 100 GB/day commitment tier ~$800-$1,500/mo; Application Insights ~$300-$600/mo; Key Vault Premium ~$50-$150/mo; B2C P2 ~$200-$800/mo; Storage accounts ~$50-$120/mo; Miscellaneous (egress, DNS, automation) ~$200-$400/mo.

Cost optimization levers: (1) 1-year Reserved Capacity on APIM Premium Canada Central (4 units) and West Europe (3 units) primary units — estimated $3,500-$4,500/mo saving vs PAYG; (2) Scale Canada East to 1 unit (minimum) during off-peak hours via scheduled Terraform pipeline if traffic patterns allow; (3) Log Analytics commitment tier pricing vs PAYG saves ~25% at 100 GB/day; (4) Defender for APIs cost (~$200/mo for estimated API surface) included in Defender for Cloud P2 bundle if already licensed; (5) Review APIM unit count quarterly using capacity metric trends — right-size before renewing reserved instances. Azure Cost Management budget alert set at 110% of monthly estimate with action group notification to CCoE FinOps team. Chargeback model: APIM costs tagged by API product and consumer subscription for showback to business units via Cost Management cost allocation rules (tag: apim-product, apim-subscription-tier).

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-apim-gateway-prod-cac
resourceGroups.purpose: Primary production APIM gateway runtime, internal VNet integration, and associated policy infrastructure for North American region
resourceGroups.resources: Azure API Management Premium (primary unit), APIM private DNS zone contributor, diagnostic settings, managed identity for Key Vault access

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-apim-gateway-prod-cae
resourceGroups.purpose: Secondary production APIM gateway unit for North American geo-redundancy and active-active failover
resourceGroups.resources: Azure API Management Premium (secondary/DR unit linked to primary service), regional subnet delegation, NSG, UDR

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-apim-gateway-prod-weu
resourceGroups.purpose: European region APIM gateway unit satisfying EU data residency requirements for McCain's European manufacturing and partner integrations
resourceGroups.resources: Azure API Management Premium (West Europe gateway unit), regional VNet subnet, NSG, ExpressRoute local peering

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-apim-gateway-prod-aue
resourceGroups.purpose: Asia-Pacific region APIM gateway unit for McCain's APAC manufacturing sites and retail partners
resourceGroups.resources: Azure API Management Premium (Australia East gateway unit), regional VNet subnet, NSG

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-apim-shared-prod-cac
resourceGroups.purpose: Shared services for the API Gateway pattern including Key Vault, Application Insights, Log Analytics workspace, and developer portal storage
resourceGroups.resources: Azure Key Vault (Premium tier), Application Insights workspace-based, Log Analytics Workspace, Storage Account (developer portal media), Managed Identity

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-frontdoor-global
resourceGroups.region: Global (deployed via Front Door resource model)
resourceGroups.purpose: Azure Front Door Premium profile providing global WAF, anycast routing, and TLS termination for all APIM gateway endpoints
resourceGroups.resources: Azure Front Door Premium profile, WAF Policy (Prevention mode, OWASP 3.2), custom domains, origin groups pointing to regional APIM gateway endpoints, DDoS Network Protection plan link

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-b2c-identity-prod
resourceGroups.purpose: Azure AD B2C tenant-level resources for consumer and partner identity federation including user flows, custom policies, and application registrations
resourceGroups.resources: Azure AD B2C tenant (linked), B2C app registrations (APIM service principal, partner federation apps), Key Vault reference for B2C signing keys

resourceGroups.name: rg-mccain-apim-gateway-nonprod-cac
resourceGroups.purpose: Non-production (Dev and Staging) APIM gateway instances with reduced capacity for development and integration testing
resourceGroups.resources: Azure API Management Developer tier (Dev), Azure API Management Standard v2 tier (Staging), Application Insights (nonprod), Key Vault (nonprod)

highAvailability.drStrategy:
RTO: 15 minutes (regional APIM gateway unit failure — Front Door reroutes automatically, no manual action); 4 hours (primary APIM control plane failure requiring restore from backup). RPO: 0 minutes for API traffic routing (multi-region active-active); 24 hours for APIM configuration changes (daily backup cadence — mitigated by Git source of truth with near-zero RPO for IaC-defined configurations).

DR Runbook (documented in api-gateway-operations-runbook-v2.1): Scenario 1 — Canada Central gateway unit failure: Automated (Front Door health probe triggers; Canada East unit absorbs traffic; alert fires; CCoE investigates within 15 min SLA). Scenario 2 — Full Canada Central region failure: Canada East becomes primary for North America; CCoE scales Canada East from 2 to 4 units via Terraform pipeline (estimated 25 minutes); B2C token issuance continues from global B2C service unaffected. Scenario 3 — APIM configuration plane corruption: Restore from Storage backup to new APIM instance in Canada East; update Front Door origin to point to new instance; estimated 4-hour RTO. DR drill conducted quarterly as part of McCain's BCP testing program — documented in DR drill reports submitted to ISO 27001 audit evidence package.

Self-hosted gateways at manufacturing sites operate in disconnected mode for up to 24 hours if cloud connectivity is lost — local API routing continues using last-known configuration cache, ensuring OT/MES system continuity during WAN outages.
highAvailability.geoRedundancy: Multi-region active-active deployment: APIM Premium service spans 4 regions (Canada Central, Canada East, West Europe, Australia East) as a single APIM service instance with distributed gateway units. Each gateway unit serves its regional traffic independently — a region failure causes Front Door to route traffic to the next nearest healthy region within seconds (AFD health probe interval: 30 seconds, unhealthy threshold: 3 consecutive failures). Front Door origin groups configured with weighted routing: Canada Central (weight: 100), Canada East (weight: 50, used for overflow and failover), West Europe (weight: 100, EU traffic only via geo-routing rule), Australia East (weight: 100, APAC traffic only). EU data residency enforced: AFD routing rule matches source geography Europe → origin group containing only West Europe gateway unit — EU API traffic never routed to non-EU regions even during partial West Europe outage (EU consumers experience elevated latency from Canada Central fallback only if West Europe unit is completely unavailable and operator explicitly enables cross-region failover via override). APIM developer portal is published to all regions from the Canada Central master configuration — portal availability maintained even if primary control plane is degraded. B2C is globally available by design with geo-redundant token issuance.
highAvailability.backupStrategy: APIM configuration backup: Daily automated backup of APIM service configuration to Azure Storage (GRS) using APIM Backup REST API triggered by Azure Automation runbook. Backup includes: API definitions, policies, products, subscriptions, users, groups, named values (Key Vault references only — actual secret values not exported). Backup retention: 30 days. Backup tested monthly via restore to APIM nonprod (staging) instance and automated validation of API policy parity. Key Vault: Geo-redundant by design (keys replicated to region pair — Canada East for Canada Central KV). Soft-delete 90 days + purge protection ensures secrets recoverable after accidental deletion. Log Analytics: 90-day hot retention; 2-year cold archive in Azure Storage GRS. Azure Storage accounts (developer portal, archive): GRS replication with 99.99999999999999% (16 nines) durability. APIM API definitions also version-controlled in GitHub (McCain platform repo) via CI/CD pipeline — Git is the source of truth; backup is a safety net.
highAvailability.availabilityZones: Azure API Management Premium deployed with Availability Zone support enabled in Canada Central (zones 1, 2, 3) and West Europe (zones 1, 2, 3). AZ deployment requires APIM Premium with minimum 3 units per region to guarantee one unit per zone — Canada Central primary is configured with 4 units (zones 1,2,3,1 distribution) and West Europe with 3 units (one per zone). Zone-redundant deployment provides 99.99% SLA and protects against single datacenter failure within the region. Canada East APIM unit deployed without AZ (Canada East has limited AZ availability) — functions as geo-redundant secondary rather than AZ-redundant primary. Azure Front Door is inherently global with anycast routing across 120+ PoPs — no AZ configuration required. Key Vault is zone-redundant by default within each region. Log Analytics workspace data is replicated across zones automatically.

complianceMapping.guardrail: All APIs must enforce OAuth2 authentication at the gateway (Security — Required)
complianceMapping.azureControl: Azure Policy: Require-APIM-For-External-APIs (custom) + APIM built-in policy: validate-jwt inbound policy on all API operations; Azure Policy audit rule checking APIM APIs for presence of authentication policy; Defender for APIs anomaly detection for unauthenticated API calls
complianceMapping.implementation: Deploy Azure Policy definition 'Require-APIM-For-External-APIs' (referenced in Implementation Assets) at 'McCain-Platform' management group scope with 'Deny' effect — prevents creation of any public-facing App Service, API App, or Function App without APIM in front. Within APIM, enforce validate-jwt policy at the global '<all APIs>' policy scope in APIM so it cannot be omitted at individual API level — individual APIs can only restrict further (add claims validation) but cannot remove JWT validation. Policy template stored in apim-gateway-config-v2.1.yaml and deployed via Terraform module. Monthly compliance report generated from Azure Policy compliance dashboard and included in SOC 2 Type II evidence package as control evidence for CC6.1 (Logical Access Controls).

complianceMapping.guardrail: Rate-limiting must be configured per API product tier (Performance — Required)
complianceMapping.azureControl: APIM rate-limit-by-key and quota-by-key policies applied at product scope; Azure Monitor alert on quota exhaustion; APIM product tiers: Free (10 req/min, 1000/day), Standard (100 req/min, 50,000/day), Premium (1000 req/min, unlimited/day), Partner (500 req/min, custom quota by contract); Azure Policy custom rule auditing APIM products for presence of rate-limit policy
complianceMapping.implementation: APIM product policy templates defined in apim-gateway-config-v2.1.yaml with tier-specific rate-limit-by-key values using subscription key as counter key. Product policy applied at product scope (not API scope) to enforce consistently across all APIs in the product. CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions, referenced Terraform module) validates product policy XML schema before deployment — pipeline fails if rate-limit policy is absent from any product definition. Quota exhaustion alerts (P2 severity) notify API product owners to upgrade consumer subscription tier or investigate abuse. Rate-limit counters stored in APIM internal cache (distributed across gateway units via service bus sync) — consistent enforcement across multi-region units.

complianceMapping.guardrail: All APIs must be registered in the API catalog before production deployment (Governance — Required)
complianceMapping.azureControl: Azure Policy: Deny-APIM-API-Without-Required-Tags (custom) requiring tags: api-owner, api-domain, api-version, data-classification, api-product; APIM developer portal as catalog UI; GitHub branch protection requiring CCoE review of API definition PR before merge to main; Azure DevOps pipeline gate: APIM API existence check before backend deployment
complianceMapping.implementation: API registration workflow: (1) API team creates OpenAPI spec and submits PR to McCain platform repo; (2) CCoE Integration Engineering reviews spec for naming convention, versioning, security policy compliance, and data classification tagging; (3) On PR merge, GitHub Actions pipeline deploys API definition to APIM staging via Terraform (terraform-azure-apim-gateway module); (4) Integration test suite validates JWT enforcement and rate-limiting in staging; (5) Production deployment gate requires APIM staging API validation to pass and CCoE sign-off. Azure Policy ensures all APIM APIs carry required tags — audit mode in nonprod, deny mode in production. Developer portal serves as the live API catalog with auto-generated documentation from OpenAPI specs — all registered APIs appear in portal automatically post-deployment.

complianceMapping.guardrail: APIs must support at least one prior version during deprecation window (Versioning — Recommended)
complianceMapping.azureControl: APIM API versioning feature (URL path-based versioning: /v1, /v2); APIM API version sets configuration; Azure Monitor Log Analytics weekly query detecting deprecated APIs still receiving traffic; Azure Policy audit (not deny) checking APIM API version sets for N-1 version presence
complianceMapping.implementation: APIM API version sets configured for all APIs at initial deployment. URL path versioning (/api/v{n}/resource) enforced via APIM URL template convention in API definitions. Deprecation process: (1) New version (v2) deployed alongside existing (v1); (2) v1 marked 'Deprecated' in APIM API settings — developer portal shows deprecation notice and sunset date; (3) APIM policy on v1 adds 'Deprecation' response header with sunset date; (4) Weekly Log Analytics query (APIMDeprecatedVersionTraffic) monitors v1 call volume — if > 0 calls in final 2 weeks before sunset, API owner notified; (5) v1 removed only after 0-traffic confirmation or sunset date, whichever is later. Recommended severity means non-compliance generates advisory finding (not deployment block) — tracked in monthly API governance report to CCoE. Deprecation schedule maintained in APIM API description field and developer portal changelog.

complianceMapping.guardrail: Backend services must not be directly accessible — gateway is the sole ingress (Security — Required, derived from Security Consideration 3)
complianceMapping.azureControl: Azure Policy: Deny-Public-Endpoint-For-Backend-Services (custom, blocks public IP on App Service, Functions, AKS load balancers in application subscriptions); NSG rules denying inbound traffic except from APIM subnet CIDR; APIM internal VNet mode ensuring gateway runtime is on private network; Network Watcher flow logs verifying no direct-to-backend traffic
complianceMapping.implementation: Network controls enforced at three layers: (1) Azure Policy 'Deny-Public-Endpoint-For-Backend-Services' at application subscription management group scope — prevents application teams from accidentally exposing backends publicly; (2) NSG on backend service subnets explicitly allows inbound on service port only from APIM gateway subnet CIDRs (10.10.1.0/27, 10.11.1.0/27, 10.20.1.0/27, 10.30.1.0/27) and denies all other inbound; (3) APIM backend entities configured with private endpoint URLs (privatelink hostnames) — no public DNS entries exist for backend services. Quarterly network access review (part of ISO 27001 A.9.2.5 control) uses Network Watcher traffic analytics to verify no traffic flows directly from internet or consumers to backend service IPs — any anomaly triggers P1 security incident. ExpressRoute-connected manufacturing systems access APIM gateway via private VNet routing — on-premises traffic also flows through APIM gateway, not directly to cloud backends.

complianceMapping.guardrail: API keys must be rotated every 90 days via Key Vault (Security — Required, derived from Security Consideration 4)
complianceMapping.azureControl: Azure Key Vault rotation policy (RotationPolicy) with rotationDays: 90 on all API key secrets; Key Vault EventGrid event 'SecretNearExpiry' (30-day warning) triggers Azure Function to notify consumers; Key Vault EventGrid event 'SecretExpired' triggers emergency rotation workflow; Defender for Key Vault monitors for rotation policy bypass attempts
complianceMapping.implementation: Key Vault rotation policy configured on all APIM subscription key secrets with: rotationDays: 90, notifyDaysBeforeExpiry: 30. Rotation triggers Azure Function (func-mccain-apim-keyrotation) which: (1) Generates new key value; (2) Updates Key Vault secret with new version; (3) APIM named value (Key Vault reference) automatically picks up new version on next cache refresh (TTL: 4 hours) or immediate refresh via APIM Management API call from Function; (4) Old key version remains valid for 7-day overlap period to prevent rotation-induced consumer outage (dual-key slot mechanism); (5) Consumer notification email sent via APIM notification templates 30 days before rotation. Rotation events logged to Key Vault audit log → Log Analytics → SOC 2 evidence. For APIM primary/secondary subscription keys: primary rotated on day 90, secondary rotated on day 97 (staggered) to ensure at least one valid key always present. Annual penetration test includes key rotation workflow validation.

complianceMapping.guardrail: WAF rules must be enabled on Azure Front Door for OWASP Top 10 protection (Security — Required, derived from Security Consideration 5)
complianceMapping.azureControl: Azure Front Door Premium WAF Policy in Prevention mode; OWASP CRS 3.2 managed ruleset; Azure Policy: Deny-FrontDoor-Without-WAF (built-in: Azure/policy/blob/master/built-in-policies/policyDefinitions/CDN/AFD_WAF_Enabled_Audit.json) in Deny effect; Microsoft Defender for Cloud recommendation 'Web application firewall should be enabled for Azure Front Door' monitored in secure score
complianceMapping.implementation: WAF Policy (waf-mccain-apim-prod) deployed via Terraform in Prevention mode at initial provisioning — Terraform module includes WAF policy as a required resource with no option to disable. Azure Policy 'Deny-FrontDoor-Without-WAF' assigned at 'McCain-Corp' management group root — ensures any new Front Door profile in any McCain subscription must have WAF associated. OWASP CRS 3.2 with all rule groups enabled; exclusion list maintained in APIM operations runbook (api-gateway-operations-runbook-v2.1) with business justification for each exclusion reviewed quarterly. WAF metrics (blocked requests, rule triggers) monitored via Log Analytics with P2 alert on >5x baseline block rate. Monthly WAF rule effectiveness review: KQL query joins WAF block logs with APIM gateway logs to identify legitimate API calls incorrectly blocked (false positives) — tuned exclusions submitted as PR to Terraform module. Annual external red team assessment includes WAF bypass testing.

azureDesign.subscriptionModel: The API Gateway pattern is deployed across McCain's enterprise Management Group hierarchy under the 'McCain-Corp' root Management Group. The production APIM workload resides in a dedicated 'sub-mccain-apim-prod' subscription under the 'McCain-Platform' management group child node, isolating gateway infrastructure from application workload subscriptions and enabling independent policy scope, quota limits, and cost reporting. A parallel 'sub-mccain-apim-nonprod' subscription under 'McCain-Platform' hosts Dev and Staging environments with reduced SKU sizes. The hub connectivity subscription ('sub-mccain-hub-connectivity') owns all ExpressRoute circuits, Azure Front Door profiles, and shared DNS private zones, and is peered to the APIM spoke subscription via VNet peering through the hub. Azure AD B2C tenants are provisioned in a separate 'sub-mccain-b2c-prod' subscription aligned to the 'McCain-Identity' management group node to isolate consumer identity from corporate Entra ID. Azure Policy assignments at the 'McCain-Platform' management group level enforce guardrails including 'Require-APIM-For-External-APIs', 'Deny-Public-Endpoint-For-Backend-Services', 'Enforce-TLS-1.2-Minimum', and 'Require-DiagnosticSettings-On-APIM'. Management Group-level RBAC assigns the CCoE Integration Engineering team the 'API Management Service Contributor' role, while application teams receive 'API Management Service Reader' for catalog browsing and 'API Management API Contributor' for API definition publishing only.
